summary: Identified reentrancy vulnerability in withdraw function with CEI pattern violation and missing guard.

evidence.description: Function transfers value before updating state
No reentrancy guard detected on payable function

graph_queries.query: FIND functions WHERE visibility IN [public, external] AND has_operation TRANSFERS_VALUE_OUT
graph_queries.rationale: Identify all value transfer functions as reentrancy candidates

graph_queries.query: FIND functions WHERE operation_sequence CONTAINS [TRANSFERS_VALUE_OUT, WRITES_USER_BALANCE] AND has_reentrancy_guard = false

gaps.description: Unable to determine if external contract has callback mechanism

recommendations.action: Add OpenZeppelin ReentrancyGuard to withdraw function
recommendations.rationale: Prevents reentrant calls during state-changing operations

recommendations.action: Move balance update before external call (CEI pattern)
recommendations.rationale: Ensures state consistency even if reentrancy occurs

uncertainties.reason: Cannot analyze receiver contract without full context

creative_hypotheses.hypothesis: Attacker can drain contract by recursively calling withdraw before balance update
attack_path: Attacker deploys malicious contract with fallback function
Attacker calls withdraw with malicious contract as receiver
Fallback function calls withdraw again before balance update
Process repeats until contract balance depleted
